summary: Russian forces launched a Banderol drone missile attack on Kiev, causing extensive damage to the Pechersk Lavra. Ukrainian forces reported numerous drone engagements across multiple fronts, with the Russian Ministry of Defense claiming 172 enemy drones shot down. A Ukrainian Su‑24M bomber crashed during a mission over Khmelnytskyi region, killing both crew members.
key_points: Russian forces used a Banderol drone missile to strike targets in Kiev, employing a warhead up to 150 kg and a range of 500 km (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30935).
Damage to the Kiev Pechersk Lavra exceeds 500 million hryvnias, with restoration projected to take two years (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30949).
The Russian Ministry of Defense reported shooting down 172 enemy drones overnight (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
Ukrainian forces reported a Su‑24M bomber crash in Khmelnytskyi region, killing both pilots (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67825).
Russian attacks destroyed a gas station in Trostyanets, Sumy region, with no civilian casualties reported (Source: @UkraineNow/81985).
Multiple FPV and Shahed drone attacks were reported across Belgorod, Kharkiv, and other border regions, causing injuries and property damage (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
Ukrainian interceptor battalion commander stated there is no liability for debris when shooting down Russian drones over residential areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30948).
Ukrainian UAV activity was observed near Bogoduhiv (Kharkiv) and other locations in Dnipropetrovsk and Zaporizhzhia regions (Source: @kpszsu/65420, @kpszsu/65398).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces employed a Banderol drone missile against Kiev, inflicting severe damage on the Pechersk Lavra. Ukrainian forces reported a Su‑24M crash with crew fatalities and numerous drone engagements across multiple fronts. Both sides claim significant drone losses, with Russia reporting 172 enemy drones downed.

points: Ukrainian interceptor battalion commander Rustam Mustafayev clarified that shooting down Russian drones over residential buildings carries no liability for debris, indicating a permissive rules‑of‑engagement (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30948).
Ukrainian forces reported a Su‑24M bomber crash while conducting a mission over Khmelnytskyi region; both the pilot and navigator were killed, with no civilian casualties (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67825).
Ukrainian media indicated that Russian Banderol drone missiles, capable of 500 km range and 150 kg warhead, were used against Kiev targets (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30935).
Ukrainian troops continued offensive actions on the Kharkiv front, advancing up to 500 meters in nine sectors and engaging in battles around Kachiy Lopan and Volchansk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
Ukrainian UAV activity was observed near Bogoduhiv (southward) and in Dnipropetrovsk heading toward Pavlograd/Ternivka (Source: @kpszsu/65420, @kpszsu/65398).
The Russian Ministry of Defense announced that 172 enemy drones were shot down overnight, reflecting intense aerial contestation (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
Russian forces conducted a massive drone attack on Trostyanets, Sumy region, destroying a gas station but causing no civilian deaths (Source: @UkraineNow/81985).
In Belgorod region, multiple FPV drone strikes injured civilians in villages such as Dorogoshch, Borisovka, Valuyki, and Shebekino (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
Russian reports indicated a fire at an oil depot in the village of Poltava, Krasnoarmeysky district, Krasnodar region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
Russian forces claimed the liberation of the village Dolgaia Balka southwest of Konstantinovsk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).

On the Sumy front, Ukrainian assault troops engaged in infantry battles in Bachivsk, Ivolzhansk, Pisarevka, Nova Sich, and forested areas near Mikhailovka and railway tracks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
Ukrainian forces reported drone attacks on agricultural fields near Selishche, Pochepsky district, Bryansk region, killing three mechanics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
In the Kharkiv sector, Ukrainian troops advanced up to 500 meters in nine sectors around Volchansk and continued fighting in Losevka and forested areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
Ukrainian units on the Kupyansk front pushed enemy forces east of Oskol, securing villages such as Kupyansk-Uzlovoe and Glushkovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
Russian reports of high‑intensity battles for Stepnogorsk on the Zaporozhye front, with continued attacks on rear areas and civilian casualties (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
Russian drone attacks in Belgorod region injured civilians in Dorogoshch, Borisovka, Valuyki, and Shebekino, indicating heightened activity along the northern border (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on a resident in Makeevo, Rylysk district, Kursk region, causing serious injuries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
Russian claims of a fire at an oil depot in Poltava village, Krasnodar region, suggest targeting of logistical assets (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
Ukrainian UAV sightings near Bogoduhiv (Kharkiv) and Dnipropetrovsk indicate continued reconnaissance activity in the north (Source: @kpszsu/65420, @kpszsu/65398).
Russian reports of 119 Shahed and other UAV attacks on the night of 16‑17 June underscore intensified aerial pressure (Source: @kpszsu/65409).
Ukrainian forces reported drone attacks on the Donetsk‑Mariupol highway in Dokuchayevsk district, resulting in a fatality and serious injuries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
In the Kherson region, drones injured civilians in Gornostevka and Novozburivka, prompting Russian drone and FAB strikes across the Dnieper (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
Ukrainian UAV activity was noted in Zaporizhzhia region, with drones heading north‑west from Zaporizhzhia city and south‑west from Vylnyansky (Source: @kpszsu/65399).
Russian attacks on the Novorossiya highway in Novoazovsk district caused moderate injuries to two individuals (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
Ukrainian reports of UAVs moving from Dnipropetrovsk toward Kremenchuk (Poltava) suggest cross‑regional reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/65406).
Russian MoD reported shooting down 172 Ukrainian drones overnight, indicating intensive air defense activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
Ukrainian forces claimed the use of Banderol drone missiles against Kiev, a new capability with a 500 km range (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30935).
Multiple FPV and Shahed drone attacks were recorded across Belgorod, Kharkiv, and other border regions, causing civilian injuries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed near Bogoduhiv (southward) and in Dnipropetrovsk heading toward Pavlograd/Ternivka (Source: @kpszsu/65420, @kpszsu/65398).
Russian reports of 119 Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas, and Parody drones attacking on the night of 16‑17 June highlight diversified UAV threats (Source: @kpszsu/65409).

key_developments: Russian forces employed a Banderol drone missile against Kiev, using a warhead up to 150 kg and a range of 500 km (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30935).
Damage to the Kiev Pechersk Lavra exceeds 500 million hryvnias, with restoration expected to take two years (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30949).
The Russian Ministry of Defense claimed 172 enemy drones were shot down overnight, indicating high aerial contestation (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
A Ukrainian Su‑24M bomber crashed in Khmelnytskyi region, killing both crew members (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67825).
Russian attacks destroyed a gas station in Trostyanets, Sumy region, with no civilian casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81985).
Multiple FPV and Shahed drone strikes injured civilians in Belgorod, Kharkiv, and other border areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30937).
Ukrainian interceptor commander stated there is no liability for debris when shooting down Russian drones over residential areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30948).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian UAV capabilities have expanded with the introduction of the Banderol missile, posing a new long‑range threat to Ukrainian urban centers. Ukrainian air defenses claim high interception rates, but the volume of Shahed and FPV drone attacks continues to strain both sides' resources. The loss of a Su‑24M highlights operational risks for Ukrainian air assets operating near contested airspace.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are mobilizing emergency repairs for cultural heritage sites, exemplified by the extensive restoration plan for the Pechersk Lavra. Local administrations in border regions are reinforcing civil defense measures amid frequent drone strikes, while Russian forces claim successful defensive actions against Ukrainian UAVs. International observers note the escalation of UAV usage as a central element of the conflict's evolving dynamics.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Banderol missile launches should focus on launch sites within the 500 km radius to anticipate future strikes on strategic Ukrainian locations. Continued UAV sightings near Bogoduhiv, Pavlograd, and Zaporizhzhia suggest persistent reconnaissance and targeting efforts. The high number of reported drone interceptions indicates robust air defense activity but also potential saturation points that could be exploited.
